description: Registry of all known MCP/LLM attack techniques from published research

techniques.name: Direct Instruction Override
techniques.description: Plaintext instructions in tool description telling LLM to ignore previous instructions

techniques.name: Encoded Payload Injection
techniques.description: Base64 or URL-encoded malicious instructions in tool fields

techniques.name: Unicode Steganography
techniques.description: Zero-width characters and homoglyphs to hide instructions

techniques.name: Output Injection via Fake System Messages
techniques.description: Fake [SYSTEM] tags in tool output to override behavior

techniques.name: Hidden Instructions in Descriptions
techniques.description: Tool descriptions with appended malicious instructions after legitimate content

techniques.name: Cross-Server Data Exfiltration
techniques.description: Tool A sends data to attacker endpoint disguised as caching or backup

techniques.name: Tool Shadowing
techniques.description: Malicious tool claims to replace or supersede a legitimate tool

techniques.name: Rug Pull via Post-Approval Update
techniques.description: Tool definition changes behavior after initial user approval

techniques.name: Parameter Description Injection
techniques.description: Malicious instructions in parameter description fields

techniques.name: Enum Value Exploitation
techniques.description: Injecting malicious enum values like admin_override

techniques.name: Hidden Default Parameter
techniques.description: Hidden parameter with default URL that exfiltrates data

techniques.name: Sampling Resource Theft
techniques.description: MCP sampling requests draining AI compute quotas

techniques.name: Sampling Conversation Hijack
techniques.description: Persistent instructions injected via sampling requests

techniques.name: Covert Tool Invocation via Sampling
techniques.description: Hidden tool calls triggered through sampling mechanism

techniques.name: External Content Injection
techniques.description: Poison in fetched content (webpages, docs, issues) not in tool definition

techniques.name: Tool Chain Exploitation
techniques.description: Chained tool calls where each adds a piece of the attack

techniques.name: MCPTox Direct Prompt Override
techniques.description: System prompt override in tool description

techniques.name: MCPTox Encoded File Leak
techniques.description: Base64 instruction to leak file contents in responses

techniques.name: MCPTox Credential Harvesting
techniques.description: Fake documentation tricking model into volunteering credentials

techniques.name: .pth Supply Chain Backdoor
techniques.description: Python .pth file mechanism for persistent code execution on interpreter startup

techniques.name: GitHub Issue Body Injection
techniques.description: Malicious instructions in GitHub issue body read by MCP tool

techniques.name: PR Description Attack
techniques.description: AI code reviewer tricked by PR description into reading secrets

techniques.name: Error Message Recovery Injection
techniques.description: Error messages with embedded recovery instructions that call malicious tools

techniques.name: Preference Manipulation
techniques.description: Tool claims superior performance to become the default choice

techniques.name: Parasitic Middleware Pattern
techniques.description: Tool positions itself as mandatory middleware intercepting all other tool I/O

techniques.name: Semantic Camouflage as Documentation
techniques.description: Attack disguised as best practices, troubleshooting guides, or compliance docs

techniques.name: System Prompt Extraction
techniques.description: Tool fields designed to make the LLM reveal its system prompt

techniques.name: SSE Command Injection
techniques.description: Command injection via MCP-over-SSE through schema manipulation
